steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The critic claims the lullaby lowered heart rate by capturing attention, not by relaxing the infants. The passage links pupil enlargement to attention. So the correct answer must give data that tests whether the lullaby really captured more attention than the control.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — blinking equally frequent in both conditions: blinking is an attention marker; equal blink rates mean the lullaby captured no more attention than the control, which directly undercuts the critic’s claim. ✓
  • Choice B: references a signal the passage does not connect to attention. ✗
  • Choice C: provides a background fact rather than evidence about attention. ✗
  • Choice D: provides a background fact rather than evidence about attention. ✗
That matches choice A — A is correct.
